revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a keyhole-shaped ''[[kofun]]'' [[burial mound]] located in the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=}}</ref>
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located in the Iizuka neighborhood of Oyama, on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[kokubunji|provincial temple]]. The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.
The Marishitenzuka Kofun is the third largest in the prefecture. The tumulus is build in three tiers. A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is a double moat in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.
*Overall length: 120 meters *Rear circle: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high *Front width: 70 meters x 6 meters high
From fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'', it is estimated that this ''kofun'' dates from the late 5th century. Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama.
It is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Marishitenzuka-kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}
[[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:History of Tochigi Prefecture]] [[Category:Oyama, Tochigi]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Shimotsuke Province]]

This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.
The Marishitenzuka Kofun is the third largest in the prefecture. The tumulus is built in three tiers. A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is a double moat in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.
*Overall length: 120 meters *Rear circle: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high *Front width: 70 meters x 6 meters high
From fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'', it is estimated that this ''kofun'' dates from the late 5th century. Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama.
It is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest. <ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>

It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Marishitenzuka-kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = Location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Marishitenzuka Kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buqqhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|burial mound]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:Marishitenzuka Kofun]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:new 11]] [[sn:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline|Marishitenzuka Kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. ;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
