revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Marishitenzuka Kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Oyama, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Marishitenzuka Kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|36|22|20.39|N|139|48|21.31|E|region:JP-09_type:landmark|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Marishitenzuka Kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>

The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[en:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[ja:摩利支天塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17210244]] Wikidata name: Marishitenzuka-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.372331|N|139.805919|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama, Tochigi]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Oyama, Tochigi|Oyama]] in [[:en:Tochigi Prefecture|Tochigi Prefecture]] in the northern [[:en:Kantō region|Kantō region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern [[:en:Kantō Plain|Kantō Plain]]. This area was the central region of ancient [[:en:Shimotsuke Province|Shimotsuke Province]], and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and [[:en:Provincial temple|Provincial temple]] (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to [[:en:Marici (Buddhism)|Marishi-ten]] is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from [[:en:Oyama Station|Oyama Station]] on the [[:en:Tōhoku Shinkansen|Tōhoku Shinkansen]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[en:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[ja:摩利支天塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[en:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[ja:摩利支天塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[en:Marishitenzuka Kofun]][[ja:摩利支天塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17210244]] Wikidata name: Marishitenzuka-kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.372331|N|139.805919|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain}}. This area was the central region of ancient {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}, and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple}} (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to {{ill|Marici (Buddhism)|en|Marici (Buddhism)|WD=Q3273446|lt=Marishi-ten}} is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Oyama Station|en|Oyama Station|WD=Q801312|lt=Oyama Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Marishitenzuka Kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain}}. This area was the central region of ancient {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province}}, and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple}} (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to {{ill|Marici (Buddhism)|en|Marici (Buddhism)|WD=Q3273446|lt=Marishi-ten}} is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Oyama Station|en|Oyama Station|WD=Q801312|lt=Oyama Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Oyama Station|en|Oyama Station|WD=Q801312|lt=Oyama Station|1=Oyama Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.372331|N|139.805919|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama, Tochigi|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama|1=Oyama, Tochigi}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.

It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}. This area was the central region of ancient {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple|1=Provincial temple}} (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to {{ill|Marici (Buddhism)|en|Marici (Buddhism)|WD=Q3273446|lt=Marishi-ten|1=Marici (Buddhism)}} is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Oyama Station|en|Oyama Station|WD=Q801312|lt=Oyama Station|1=Oyama Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.372331|N|139.805919|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama, Tochigi|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|WD=Q374511|lt=Oyama|1=Oyama, Tochigi}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|WD=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|WD=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|WD=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}. This area was the central region of ancient {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|WD=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|WD=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple|1=Provincial temple}} (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to {{ill|Marici (Buddhism)|en|Marici (Buddhism)|WD=Q3273446|lt=Marishi-ten|1=Marici (Buddhism)}} is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Oyama Station|en|Oyama Station|WD=Q801312|lt=Oyama Station|1=Oyama Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|WD=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons category-inline|Marishitenzuka Kofun}} * [https://www.tochinavi.net/spot/home/?id=17309 Tochigi Tourist Information] {{in lang|ja}} * [https://www.city.oyama.tochigi.jp/site/korona/207228.html Oyama city home page] {{in lang|ja}}

It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|qid=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}. This area was the central region of ancient {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|qid=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple|1=Provincial temple}} (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to {{ill|Marici (Buddhism)|en|Marici (Buddhism)|qid=Q3273446|lt=Marishi-ten|1=Marici (Buddhism)}} is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Oyama Station|en|Oyama Station|qid=Q801312|lt=Oyama Station|1=Oyama Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|qid=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==Gallery== <gallery> Marishitenzuka-kofun marishitenson.JPG|Chapel to Marishi-ten on the summit of the tumulus Biwazuka-kofun and Marishitenzuka-kofun -2.JPG|Biwazuka Kofun and Marishitenzuka Kofun </gallery>
==See also== {{Portal|History|Japan}} *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Tochigi)]]

{{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name = Marishitenzuka Kofun | native_name =摩利支天塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Marishitenzuka-kofun zenkei.JPG | image_size = | alt = | caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Tochigi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Marishitenzuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|36.372331|N|139.805919|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|qid=Q374511|lt=Oyama, Tochigi|1=Oyama, Tochigi}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} | type = [[kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = late 5th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Marishitenzuka Kofun'''|摩利支天塚古墳 |}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]] located in the Iizuka neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Oyama, Tochigi|en|Oyama, Tochigi|qid=Q374511|lt=Oyama|1=Oyama, Tochigi}} in {{ill|Tochigi Prefecture|en|Tochigi Prefecture|qid=Q44843|lt=Tochigi Prefecture|1=Tochigi Prefecture}} in the northern {{ill|Kantō region|en|Kantō region|qid=Q132480|lt=Kantō region|1=Kantō region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. It received protection as a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site]] in 1978.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/138077|title=摩利支天塚古墳|trans-title= Marishitenzuka kofun |language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref> It is the third largest in the prefecture.
==Overview== The Marishitenzuka Kofun is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It is located on a narrow plateau between the Oshi and Sugata rivers, which run north and south through the northern {{ill|Kantō Plain|en|Kantō Plain|qid=Q1124865|lt=Kantō Plain|1=Kantō Plain}}. This area was the central region of ancient {{ill|Shimotsuke Province|en|Shimotsuke Province|qid=Q1053699|lt=Shimotsuke Province|1=Shimotsuke Province}}, and contains many ''kofun'' and the ruins of the [[kokufu|provincial capital]] and {{ill|Provincial temple|en|Provincial temple|qid=Q1207757|lt=Provincial temple|1=Provincial temple}} (''kokubunji''). The Marishitenzuka Kofun is located 100 meters south of the [[Biwazuka Kofun]], which is of almost identical size and orientation to the south-southwest.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
A small chapel to {{ill|Marici (Buddhism)|en|Marici (Buddhism)|qid=Q3273446|lt=Marishi-ten|1=Marici (Buddhism)}} is located on its summit, giving the tumulus its name. The tumulus was built by partially using a natural hill, and was constructed in two tiers. Fragments of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been recovered. The construction date is said to be the end of the 5th century AD based on the shape of the tumulus and the excavated ''haniwa''. The tumulus is surrounded by a moat with a width of 20 meters, which is double in places. The National Historic Site designation was expanded in 2002 to cover the remnants of the moats.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Overall length: 120 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 70 meter diameter x 10 meter high, 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 70 meters wide x 6 meters high, 2-tier
Recovered artifacts are on display at the Marishitenzuka-Biwazuka Kofun Museum in Oyama. The site is located approximately 15 minutes by car from {{ill|Oyama Station|en|Oyama Station|qid=Q801312|lt=Oyama Station|1=Oyama Station}} on the {{ill|Tōhoku Shinkansen|en|Tōhoku Shinkansen|qid=Q900665|lt=Tōhoku Shinkansen|1=Tōhoku Shinkansen}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
